title: A community event I attended as a stranger
summary: Narrate a local event — a neighbourhood meeting, a small fair, or a community gathering — that you attended without knowing anyone.
topic: A small community event I attended as a stranger
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences about a local community event you attended in a new place where you did not know anyone. Describe what happened and how you felt. Use past simple and adverbs of frequency.
hintStarters: I saw a poster on the notice board and decided to…
When I arrived, everyone seemed to know each other, so I…
A woman with a red apron came over and…
By the end of the evening, I felt…

hintVocab.meaningVi: thuộc về, là thành phần của

sampleText: Three weeks after I moved into my new flat in Porto, I saw a handwritten poster in the entrance of my building. It said there was a neighbourhood clean-up day in the park nearby, followed by food and drinks. I did not know anyone in the building yet, so I went alone. When I arrived at the park, about thirty people were already there with brooms and bags. Everyone seemed to know each other. I felt a little awkward, so I just picked up a bag and started filling it with leaves. After a few minutes, a tall man in a green jacket came over and introduced himself. His name was António and he had lived in that street for twenty years. He walked with me around the park and told me about each family and which building they were from. I did not remember most of the names but I liked listening. At the end of the clean-up, everyone sat on folding chairs and someone brought out two large pots of soup. António filled a bowl for me without asking and put it in my hands. It was simple vegetable soup but I ate the whole thing. That evening I walked home feeling less like a stranger than I had felt for weeks.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: lối vào tòa nhà của tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Everyone seemed to know each other
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Mọi người có vẻ quen biết nhau

sampleAnnotations.phrase: picked up a bag and started filling it
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nhặt một túi và bắt đầu nhặt vào đó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: had lived in that street for twenty years
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đã sống trên phố đó hai mươi năm
kể cho tôi nghe về từng gia đình
ngồi trên những chiếc ghế gấp

sampleAnnotations.phrase: filled a bowl for me without asking
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đổ súp vào bát cho tôi mà không hỏi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: less like a stranger than I had felt for weeks
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ít cảm thấy như người lạ hơn so với nhiều tuần qua

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for a state that started before the story moment
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Ở cuối bài, 'less like a stranger than I had felt for weeks' dùng past perfect ('had felt') để chỉ rằng cảm giác cô đơn đã bắt đầu trước thời điểm trong câu chuyện — nó kéo dài nhiều tuần trước khoảnh khắc đó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: At the end of the piece, 'less like a stranger than I had felt for weeks' uses past perfect ('had felt') to show that the feeling of being a stranger started before the story moment — it lasted for weeks leading up to that evening.

sampleTranslationVi: Ba tuần sau khi chuyển vào căn hộ mới ở Porto, tôi thấy một tờ áp phích viết tay ở lối vào tòa nhà. Nó nói rằng có một ngày dọn dẹp khu phố tại công viên gần đó, tiếp theo là ăn uống. Tôi chưa quen ai trong tòa nhà, nên tôi đến một mình. Khi đến công viên, đã có khoảng ba mươi người ở đó với chổi và túi. Mọi người có vẻ quen biết nhau. Tôi cảm thấy hơi lúng túng, nên chỉ nhặt một túi và bắt đầu nhặt lá vào đó. Sau vài phút, một người đàn ông cao trong áo khoác xanh lá cây đến và tự giới thiệu. Tên ông là António và ông đã sống trên phố đó hai mươi năm. Ông đi cùng tôi quanh công viên và kể cho tôi nghe về từng gia đình và họ ở tòa nhà nào. Tôi không nhớ hết các tên nhưng tôi thích nghe. Cuối buổi dọn dẹp, mọi người ngồi trên những chiếc ghế gấp và ai đó mang ra hai nồi súp lớn. António đổ súp vào bát cho tôi mà không hỏi và đặt nó vào tay tôi. Đó là món súp rau đơn giản nhưng tôi ăn hết cả bát. Buổi tối hôm đó tôi đi bộ về nhà với cảm giác ít như người lạ hơn so với nhiều tuần qua.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about seeing a poster.
options: I saw a handwritten poster at the entrance.
I see a handwritten poster at the entrance.
I was seeing a handwritten poster at the entrance.
I have seen a handwritten poster at the entrance.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'saw' is correct for a completed observation in the past. 'Was seeing' is continuous and unnatural for a brief perception.
mcQuestions.hint: Is seeing a poster an ongoing action or a brief completed event?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the correct way to say everyone seemed to know each other.
options: Everyone seemed to know each other.
Everyone seemed to knew each other.
Everyone seemed knowing each other.
Everyone were seeming to know each other.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Seem + to + infinitive' is the correct pattern. 'Seemed to knew' is wrong — after 'to', use base verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes a person who has lived somewhere for a long time?
options: He lived in that street since twenty years.
He had lived in that street for twenty years.
He has lived in that street for twenty years.
He lived in that street twenty years.
mcQuestions.explanation: In a past narrative, 'had lived for twenty years' (past perfect) correctly shows a state that lasted from before the story to the story moment. 'Since' is for a start point; 'for' is for duration.
mcQuestions.hint: In a past story, which tense shows a state that had been continuing up to that moment?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about introducing yourself.
options: He came over and introduced himself.
He came over and introduce himself.
He came over and introduced him.
He came over and was introducing himself.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Introduced himself' is the correct reflexive form of 'introduce'. 'Introduced him' would mean he introduced someone else.
mcQuestions.hint: When someone introduces their own name, what pronoun form do they use?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about picking something up and filling it.
options: I picked up a bag and started filling it with leaves.
I picked up a bag and started filling it of leaves.
I picked up a bag and started filling it for leaves.
I picked a bag up and started to fill it of leaves.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Fill something with something' is the correct collocation. 'Filling it of' and 'filling it for' are incorrect.
mcQuestions.hint: What preposition follows 'fill' when naming the material?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'without' correctly to describe an action done without hesitation?
options: He filled a bowl for me without asking.
He filled a bowl for me without to ask.
He filled a bowl for me without asked.
He filled a bowl for me without ask.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Without + verb-ing' is the correct structure. 'Without' is a preposition and must be followed by the -ing form.
mcQuestions.hint: What verb form follows a preposition like 'without'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the phrase that correctly compares how you felt before and after.
options: I felt less like a stranger than I had felt for weeks.
I felt less like a stranger than I felt for weeks.
I felt more less like a stranger than I had felt for weeks.
I felt less like a stranger as I had felt for weeks.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Less than' is the correct comparison. 'Past perfect' (had felt) shows the earlier state. 'More less' is redundant. 'As' does not introduce a comparative clause here.
mcQuestions.hint: What word introduces the comparison after 'less'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the word that best means 'feeling out of place or uncomfortable in a social situation'.
options: tiny
awkward
familiar
volunteer
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Awkward' describes a feeling of discomfort or social difficulty. 'Tiny' means very small; 'familiar' means well-known; 'volunteer' is a person who helps for free.
mcQuestions.hint: Which adjective describes feeling uncomfortable or out of place?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly says you ate something completely.
options: I ate the whole thing.
I ate the all thing.
I ate all the whole thing.
I ate the whole of thing.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The whole thing' means the entire item. 'The all thing' is incorrect; 'all the whole' is redundant.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about bringing food out for people.
options: Someone brought out two large pots of soup.
Someone bring out two large pots of soup.
Someone has brought out two large pots of soup.
Someone brought out two large pot of soup.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Brought' is the irregular past simple of 'bring'. 'Pots' must be plural to match 'two'. 'Has brought' is present perfect.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'bring'? Is 'two large pot' or 'two large pots' correct?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says you walked home in the evening?
options: That evening I walked home.
That evening I walked to home.
That evening I walked at home.
That evening I walked toward home.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Walk home' does not use a preposition. 'To home' and 'at home' are incorrect; 'toward home' is unusual in this context.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'walk home' need a preposition before 'home'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever joined a group or event where you knew no one? What made the experience easier or harder than you expected?
